config.example.yaml.body:
server:
  port: 25297
  host: "127.0.0.1"       # override to "0.0.0.0" for Cloud Run
  frontend_dir: "./web/dist"
  log_format: ""           # "json", "text", or "" (auto: json in prod, text in dev). Env: LOG_FORMAT
  log_level: "info"        # "debug", "info", "warn", "error". Env: LOG_LEVEL

database:
  driver: ""               # "postgres" | "sqlite" | "" (auto: sqlite locally, postgres otherwise)
  postgres_url: ""         # from env: DATABASE_URL
  sqlite_path: "./clawvisor.db"

vault:
  backend: "local"         # "local" | "gcp"
  local_key_file: "./vault.key"
  gcp_project: ""          # from env: GCP_PROJECT

auth:
  jwt_secret: ""           # from env: JWT_SECRET  (auto-generated locally; required in prod)
  access_token_ttl: "15m"
  refresh_token_ttl: "720h"
  max_users: 0             # from env: MAX_USERS — 0 = unlimited; set to 1 for single-user deployments

approval:
  timeout: 300             # seconds before approval request expires
  on_timeout: "fail"       # "fail" | "skip"

callback:
  allow_private_cidrs: []  # CIDRs exempt from SSRF callback blocking, e.g. ["192.168.1.0/24"]
  require_https: false     # when true, reject http:// callback URLs except for localhost. Env: CALLBACK_REQUIRE_HTTPS

task:
  default_expiry_seconds: 1800  # 30 minutes — task-scoped authorization window

# ── Service / adapter configuration ─────────────────────────────────────────
services:
  google:
    client_id: ""          # from env: GOOGLE_CLIENT_ID
    client_secret: ""

README.md.body:
Clawvisor

Your agents act. You stay in control.
The gatekeeper between your AI agents and the APIs they act on.

Get Started · CLI · Agent Integration · Dashboard · Services · Runtime Proxy · Security

--- > [!WARNING] > **Use at your own risk.** Clawvisor is experimental software under active development. It has not been audited for security. LLMs are inherently nondeterministic — we make no guarantees that policies or safety checks will behave as expected in every case. Do not use Clawvisor as your sole safeguard for sensitive data or critical systems. --- AI agents are getting good at doing things. The problem is letting them — safely. Give an agent your Gmail credentials and it can read, send, and delete anything. Refuse, and it can't help you at all. Clawvisor sits in the middle. Agents never hold credentials. Instead, they declare **tasks** describing what they need to do, the user approves the scope, and Clawvisor handles credential injection, execution, and audit logging for every request under that task.
